# Vamo

> **Vamo** is a straightforward, non opinionated, developer-centric HTTP client library for Rust.
It offers a rich array of modern features—from flexible authentication and serialization formats
to runtime compatibility and middleware support—while maintaining simplicity and ease of use.
It’s especially well-suited for Rust projects that require a lightweight, efficient HTTP client
without sacrificing control or extensibility.

## Installation

```toml
[dependencies]
deboa = { version = "0.0.9", features = ["http1", "tokio-rt"] }
```

## Quickstart

```rust
use deboa::{Client, request::get, Result};
use deboa_extras::http::serde::json::JsonBody;
use serde::Deserialize;

#[derive(Deserialize)]
struct Post {
    id: u64,
    title: String,
    body: String,
}

#[tokio::main]
async fn main() -> Result<()> {
    let client = Client::new();

    let posts: Vec<Post> = get("https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts")
        .send_with(&client)
        .await?
        .body_as(JsonBody, Post)?;

    println!("First post: {}", posts[0].title);
    Ok(())
}
```

## Features

See [vamo README](https://github.com/ararog/vamo/blob/main/README.md) and [vamo-macros README](https://github.com/ararog/vamo/blob/main/vamo-macros/README.md) for a comprehensive list of features.

## Crates

- [vamo](https://crates.io/crates/vamo)
- [vamo_macros](https://crates.io/crates/vamo_macros)

## Examples

- [blog](https://github.com/ararog/deboa/blob/main/examples/blog): A simple blog application using Vamo. This example shows how to use
use Vamo to quickly make request to fetch posts from a REST api.
It also shows how to use DeboaCatcher trait to implement a simple middleware.
